published.text:
Q: What kind of businesses do you work with?
A: Small businesses with 1-20 employees that have repetitive work eating up their time or money. That could be lead follow-up, client onboarding, reporting, scheduling, content, or anything else that runs on the same steps every time. I've worked with HVAC companies, restaurants, real estate agents, mortgage brokers, e-commerce brands, law firms, and more. If it's manual and repeatable, it can be automated.

edits.description: Opens with size qualifier (1-20 employees) — filters without excluding
"That could be..." — gives examples of automation types, not just industries
Lists industries mid-answer as proof of range, not definition of who qualifies
Closer is the actual test: "If it's manual and repeatable, it can be automated" — anyone can self-identify
Short sentences throughout (Q1 answer averages ~15 words/sentence)

opener.text: Q: What kind of businesses do you work with?
